Tinospora crispa, commonly known as Tingulonga insignis, is a climbing plant native to Southeast Asia, known for its medicinal properties and traditional uses in herbal medicine. The health benefits of Tinospora crispa include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Tinospora crispa include alkaloids, flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Tinospora crispa is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
